guarantees.requirement: Clamps x into the inclusive range [lo, hi]; the result never falls outside it.

spec.signature: clamp(x: number, lo: number, hi: number): number

guarantees.backTranslation: The clamp function takes three numbers (x, lo, hi) and returns a number that is greater than or equal to lo and less than or equal to hi.

guarantees.requirement: Returns the larger of a and b.
guarantees.discrepancy: The requirement says the function returns the LARGER of a and b, meaning the result must be >= a AND >= b (i.e., it is the maximum of the two). The contract only ensures result >= a, completely omitting the condition result >= b. A value could satisfy the contract while being less than b, which would not be the larger of the two values.
guarantees.backTranslation: The largest function takes two numbers (a, b) and returns a number that is greater than or equal to a.

gaps.reason: //@ contract present but no //@ requires/ensures to back it
